title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
summary: Introduced BERT, which uses masked language modeling (randomly masking 15% of input tokens and predicting them) and next sentence prediction to pretrain a bidirectional transformer encoder. The pretrained model is then fine-tuned with a single output layer for specific tasks. BERT set new SOTA on 11 NLP benchmarks simultaneously, proving that large-scale pretraining followed by task-specific fine-tuning is the optimal paradigm for NLP.
key_contributions: Introduced masked language modeling (MLM) as a pretraining objective
Demonstrated that bidirectional pretraining captures richer representations than left-to-right
Established the pretrain-then-fine-tune paradigm for NLP
Set new SOTA on 11 benchmarks simultaneously with a single architecture
impact: BERT proved that a single pretrained model could dominate virtually every NLP task. It spawned an enormous family of encoder models and established the paradigm that defines modern NLP.
